messages.content:
Translate to Classical Chinese

"The 43rd Azure Emperor"
"I still feel as though I am me"

"The first documentary evidence of the phrase "United States of America" dates back to a letter from January 2, 1776, written by Stephen Moylan, a Continental Army aide to General George Washington, to Joseph Reed, Washington's aide-de-camp. Moylan expressed his desire to go "with full and ample powers from the United States of America to Spain" to seek assistance in the Revolutionary War effort. The first known publication of the phrase "United States of America" was in an anonymous essay in The Virginia Gazette newspaper in Williamsburg, on April 6, 1776.By June 1776, the name "United States of America" appeared in drafts of the Articles of Confederation and Perpetual Union, authored by John Dickinson, a Founding Father from the Province of Pennsylvania, and in the Declaration of Independence, written primarily by Thomas Jefferson and adopted by the Second Continental Congress in Philadelphia, on July 4, 1776."

「美利堅合眾國」一詞的首次具體見於紀錄，可以追溯到1776年1月2日，由康瓦斯陸軍助手史提芬·莫伊蘭致伊夫·里德（喬治·華盛頓的副官）的信件中。莫伊蘭表達了他希望以“美利堅合眾國的充分並具有充分權力”前往西班牙尋求協助革命戰爭努力的意願。關於“美利堅合眾國”一詞的首次出版是在1776年4月6日的《維吉尼亞州蓋濟報》一篇匿名文章中。到了1776年6月，《美洲邦聯和永久聯盟條款》（由賓夕法尼亞省的創始人約翰·迪金森主導起草）的草案以及《獨立宣言》（主要由托馬斯·傑斐遜撰寫並於1776年7月4日由第二屆大陸會議在費城通過）中都出現了“美利堅合眾國”一詞。
